# SL-CANON-013: hreflang-target-non-success

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-rule-docs.mjs. Do not edit by hand. -->

## Summary

A hreflang target does not return a 2xx HTTP response.

## Metadata

- Category: Canonical and hreflang (canonical-hreflang)
- Default severity: error
- Confidence: certain
- Scope: site
- Provider scope: core
- Version: 1.0.0

## Evidence Sources

- crawler
- http-header

## Required Evidence

- hreflang-target
- final-status-code

## When This Triggers

Resolve hreflang target URLs and inspect final status.

## False Positives

False positives are expected to be rare because this rule relies on
deterministic local evidence. Suppress only with a reason when the route
contract intentionally differs from the observed signal.

## How To Fix

Point hreflang to successful alternate URLs.

## Examples

Bad-case fixture reference:

- fixtures/canonical/hreflang-target-non-success

Fixed direction:

- Point hreflang to successful alternate URLs.

The generated documentation does not invent HTML, DSL, source locations,
selectors, or external observations beyond the catalog fixture references above.

- CLI, crawler, browser collector, overlay, and Next.js dev mode can share this
  rule through the common core engine.

  evidence.
